official_functions.role: Name the packet id assigned in Play clientbound state
Construct, write, and decode the chunk_batch_start empty packet body
Write and dispatch-decode the full official Play clientbound chunk_batch_start frame

invariants: The official Play clientbound table assigns chunk_batch_start immediately after chunk_batch_finished.
The official Play clientbound protocol codec dispatches the chunk_batch_start id to ClientboundChunkBatchStartPacket.
The official chunk_batch_start body is empty, encoded by StreamCodec.unit(INSTANCE).
The fixture uses official ClientboundChunkBatchStartPacket.INSTANCE, so it does not require an initialized Level, chunk storage, or game state.

The framed bytes, packet table id, body bytes, decoded packet class, singleton decode checks, Play clientbound packet table, and remaining decode byte count are generated by the official jar-backed answer artifact.

This contract does not prove chunk batch pacing behavior, chunk-load start semantics, chunk-load completion, initialized game state, spawn readiness, world load, render readiness, or client-load completion.
test_topology_note: rust_test_target names the current case adapter only; the manifest owns the runnable test topology.
stop_boundary: Do not use this framed dispatch/decode contract to infer chunk batch pacing behavior, chunk-load start semantics, chunk-load completion, initialized game state, spawn readiness, world load, render readiness, or client-load completion.
